title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 January 2025.

items.title: **Tetsuhisa Seko**, Nippon Ichi (NIS) president, has died at age 54
Ex-**Gearbox Publishing** devs form new publisher Cooldown Games
bullets: Co-founder and CEO Steve Gibson said the plan is as it was at Gearbox: bring "promising projects" to the game market and continue working with his fellow alums after Gearbox Software was sold off to Take-Two last year

Helping Gibson start up the new company are chief officers Matt Charles (product), Erick Cobbs (business), Jackson Hardesty (operations), Jeff Skal (marketing), Jimmy Sieben (technology), and Brad Schliesser (revenue).

Cooldown's overall aim is to publish titles "where you don’t feel like you’re falling behind if you don’t play it enough." Skal noted the ongoing struggles with live-service and ongoing games trying to vie for players' time when they already have "forever games" set in stone. Cooldown-published titles, he reasoned, can serve as good in-between games instead of titles that "ask more of us than we can give."

items.title: **Savvy Games** hires Nika Nour as SVP and head of international business development
bullets: Nour will oversee international partnerships, provide advice on mergers, acquisitions, and investments

items.title: **Annapurna** appoints ex-Netflix exec Leanne Loombe as head of games
bullets: Loombe will oversee the company's publishing, development, and creative segments

items.title: **PlayStation Productions**’ next movie project is **Resident Evil**
bullets: The next movie based on Capcom’s popular survival popular video game series will supposedly be directed by Zach Cregger, the actor and writer who helmed 2022 horror film Barbarian
Major studios are currently said to be bidding for the new film produced by PlayStation Productions
